items.prompt: How does this chapter distinguish ethics from privacy?
choices: Ethics and privacy are identical legal terms
Ethics guides responsible collection and use; privacy focuses on individual control over personal data
Privacy applies only to government agencies
Ethics applies only after a model is deployed
items.explain: Ethics asks who is harmed or helped and whether uses are fair and accountable; privacy focuses on consent and control over personal information.

items.prompt: A team can be legally compliant yet still ethically weak.
items.explain: The chapter notes that opaque or discriminatory uses can fail ethics even when minimum legal requirements are met.

items.prompt: In Example 3.1 (wellness app), which tension is central?
choices: Product utility, consent, and partner sharing among multiple stakeholders
Whether to use differential privacy noise
How to stratify a probability sample
items.explain: Example 3.1 shows product, legal, executive, and user interests colliding in one data decision.

items.prompt: Name two stakeholder roles besides data subjects mentioned in this clip.
items.rubric: Any two of: practitioners, leaders, policymakers (or legal/compliance partners).
items.sample_answer: Practitioners who build pipelines and leaders who set objectives.
